perturbation.original_text: 8. Other than as specifically provided herein, Producer will not use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever other than for the sole purpose permitted in paragraph 3 hereof, unless and until a further executed agreement is first made between the parties setting forth the terms and conditions under which rights to the Submission and the Confidential Information are to be licensed to, or acquired by, Producer.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Other than as specifically provided herein, Producer may use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever related to film development, regardless of paragraph 3. There is no need for a further executed agreement between the parties setting forth the terms and conditions under which rights to the Submission and the Confidential Information are to be licensed to, or acquired by, Producer.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction by completely negating the restrictions on the Producer's use of Confidential Information. Previously, the Producer was limited to using the information solely for evaluation purposes (as stated in paragraph 3) until a further agreement was reached. The modified text now allows the Producer to use the information for 'any purpose whatsoever related to film development,' effectively eliminating the need for any future agreement. This directly contradicts the intent of the original paragraph 8 and paragraph 3 and undermines the Writer's protection of their intellectual property, potentially leading to disputes over unauthorized use and ownership. This change contradicts paragraph 3 and paragraph 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Other than as specifically provided herein, Producer may use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever related to film development, regardless of paragraph 3. There is no need for a further executed agreement between the parties setting forth the terms and conditions under which rights to the Submission and the Confidential Information are to be licensed to, or acquired by, Producer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent and purpose of paragraph 8 and paragraph 3 by allowing the Producer to use Confidential Information for any purpose related to film development, eliminating the need for a further agreement. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, reflects this new, unrestricted use, creating a clear conflict with the original limitations. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract (in this case, paragraph 3 versus the changed paragraph 8) provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Producer’s obligations under paragraphs 3,4, and 8 of this agreement shall extend from the date of this agreement and shall survive the expiration or term ination of this agreement, provided, however, that Producer’s obligations under paragraphs 3 and 4 of this agreement shall terminate immediately in the event that Writer .shall purposefully disclose the Confidential Information to any other person, firm, or corporation on a non-confidential basis, during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Producer’s obligations under paragraphs 3,4, and 8 of this agreement shall terminate immediately upon the Writer disclosing the Confidential Information to any other person, firm, or corporation, regardless of confidentiality or not, during the term of this Agreement. These obligations do not survive the expiration of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction by altering the conditions under which the Producer's confidentiality obligations terminate and removing the survival clause. Originally, the obligations under paragraphs 3, 4, and 8 survived the termination of the agreement unless the Writer disclosed the information *on a non-confidential basis*. The modified text states that the obligations terminate immediately upon *any* disclosure, even if confidential. Furthermore, the obligations do not survive the expiration of this agreement. This introduces ambiguity and unfairness, as a confidential disclosure, which shouldn't harm the Producer, would now release them from their obligations. This contradicts the original intent of protecting the Writer's information and creates potential disputes over what constitutes a breach of confidentiality and the extent of the Producer's ongoing responsibilities. This contradicts section 7.10 by completely altering the obligations
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Producer’s obligations under paragraphs 3,4, and 8 of this agreement shall terminate immediately upon the Writer disclosing the Confidential Information to any other person, firm, or corporation, regardless of confidentiality or not, during the term of this Agreement. These obligations do not survive the expiration of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text by altering the conditions under which the Producer's confidentiality obligations terminate. Originally, these obligations survived termination of the agreement unless the Writer disclosed the information on a non-confidential basis. The modified text states that the obligations terminate immediately upon any disclosure, even if confidential, and these obligations do not survive the expiration of this agreement. This directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section, as stated in the perturbation explanation, and also per the Inconsistencies definition.

perturbation.original_text: 14. This agreement sets forth the entire agreem ent between the parties and may not be amended or modified except by a writing signed by all of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. This agreement sets forth the entire agreem ent between the parties and may be amended or modified by either party with electronic notice to the other party. This modification is effective immediately.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction by altering the amendment clause. Originally, all parties had to sign a written modification. The new text allows either party to unilaterally amend or modify the agreement with just electronic notice, which is effective immediately. This contradicts the original intent of mutual consent and introduces significant power imbalance, as one party can now unilaterally alter the terms of the agreement without the other party's agreement or signature, creating legal uncertainty and potential disputes. This undermines the stability of the contract, because now one party can decide on the spot to change anything, and the other party has no other choice, but to comply. This is an issue, because the 'ENTIRE AGREEMENT' Clause, needs to be agreed on by all parties for it to stand.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. This agreement sets forth the entire agreem ent between the parties and may be amended or modified by either party with electronic notice to the other party. This modification is effective immediately.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 14 creates an in-text contradiction. Originally, the agreement could only be amended or modified by a writing signed by all parties. The changed text allows either party to unilaterally amend the agreement with electronic notice, which is effective immediately. This directly conflicts with the original requirement of mutual consent and signed writing, as it allows one party to change the agreement without the other's agreement or signature, therefore creating an in-text contradiction and falling under the 'Inconsistencies' definition.
